Petition To Remove Trust From Court Supervision And For Exoneration Of Court-Supervised Successor Trustees Bond And For Replacement With Non-Court Supervised Bond
Off Calendar Aug-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Siracusa explained that a non supervised bond is being requested to save on costs of filing accountings, that they need to convert this bond to one that does not require accountings and that there is no difference in the costs. The Court granted the Petition to Remove Trust from Court Supervision, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition Of Trustee To Approve Reimbursement To Lifetime Beneficiary For Emergency Travel Costs And Funeral Costs; And Petition For Instructions Regarding Distribution Of Travel Allowance With Respect To 2014
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges.

Mr. Siracusa explained that the Beneficiaries Brother passed away, that the Beneficiary receives a substantial monthly allowance of $5,000.00 plus the travel allowance, and that her rent is paid for. Mr. Siracusa also explained that the Brother was like a Father to the Beneficiary and that the Family did not have funds to pay for funeral expenses. The Court stated that the travel charges needs to come out of the 2014 travel expenses. The Court also raised the issue of dividing up future travel expenses. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Reimbursement, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Henry. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Distribute Pecuniary Bequests; To Approve Monthly Payments To Lifetime Beneficiary; For Authority To Make A Lump Sum Payment To Beneficiary; Authority To Pay Attorney Fees To Lifetime Beneficiary'S Attorney And Successor Trustee'S Attorneys And Petition For Authorization Of Periodic Payment On Account Of Fees To Professional Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Petitioner). Peter Schwartz for Asuncion Felix.

Mr. Schwartz stated that $20,000.00 was used exclusively to pay for the Daughter's wedding in the Philippines. Ms. Alavi stated that the Trustee agreed to advance the funds with a written agreement that Ms. Felix would repay it if not approved by the Court. Niether Mr. Schwartz or Ms. Alavi have the exact figures of the cost of the wedding or for the travel expenses. Ms. Alavi expressed that it would be appropriate to set guidelines and boundries on how to proceed with the Trust and provided a breakdown on the $70,000.00 request. In addition, she stated that there was no question as to the authority to make payment from the principal for health, support and education, but that the issue was the rate of disbursement. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi stated that if a set amount was not set by the Court, that it would lead to further problems down the line. The Court expressed concern with the funds being depleted and preserving the funds for the lifetime of the Beneficiary. The Court ordered that the trust pay $20,000.00 for the wedding, $15,000.00 for travel expenses, $15,000.00 to the bank account and that the Beneficiary was to pay her attorney's fees out of those funds. The request for payment of $6,000.00 a month in addition to payment of rent will not be retroactive and will start as of December 1, 2009 which is to come out of the net income, not the principal. The Court granted the petition, (signing the modified order for the same in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Oct-13-2009 Off Calendar

Petition Of Successor Trustee For Authority To Pay Fees To Settlor'S Attorney
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Former Trustee). Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Successor Trustee). Peter Schwarz for Ascuncion Felix (Objector).

The Court stated that that it had one issue with respect to the authority to pay the settlor's attorney; the $1,000.00 gift. Mr. Der stated that the $1,000.00 to Jane Mahakian as Successor Case Manager was money she advanced to Asuncion Felx which was for Mr. McClure, that it was a reimbursement, and that he didn't know what it was actually spent on. He then stated that the $1,000.00 to Janet Felix, Daughter of Asuncion Felix, was a gift from Mr. McClure to Janet on her wedding day. Mr. Der stated that he had filed a declaration today. The Court addressed the $5,000.00 for services. Mr. Der stated that they had spent a lot of time on the Carlisle issue, that they had three units to settle, that the Carlisle litigation was started by the residents of Carlisle, that they had various meetings, that Mr. Watson went to all the monthly meetings, that Mr. Watson provided them with updates, that after the Carlisle was sold there were negotiations regarding a buyback, and that his client feels the amount is ok. On behalf of the Objector, Mr. Schwarz stated that Mr. Watson represented his Mother's Trust not the McClure Trust, that they were adversaries, that they were trying to divide it, that he was never a client of the McClure Trust, and that the fees were not documented. The Fourth and Final Account and Report was taken under submission.

The Court granted the Petition Authorizing To Pay Fees To Settlor's Attorney (an order for the same was signed in open court).

Judge: David Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES

Petition For Authority To Hire Illlinois Attorney And To Sell Settlor'S Farm In Illinois And Execute The Exclusive Right To Sell Contract And Petn. Directing Petitioner To File An Ex Parte Petn. To Increase Bond Prior To The Close Of The Sale
Off Calendar May-05-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Successor Trustee).

Ms. Alavi stated that the proposal of Dale Apperlee was unsolicited, that he was recommended by William Bates and that Mr. Bates had 30 years experience handling agricultural real estate sales. In addition, she stated that Mr. Bates would assist with the taxes, closing of escrow, that his fees were based on an hourly rate of $275.00, that there was no cap and explained how the commission would be split. The Court approved the request for the $10,000.00 retainer for Mr. Bates' services. Any additional fees above the $10,000.00 amount has to be authorized by the Court. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
